data.title: Organizers seek speakers for NAMA Show 2026 across diverse sectors; call open for experts in healthcare, hospitality, education, and retail.
data.description: A business event called The NAMA Show 2026 in Arlington, Virginia, is seeking proposals from professionals in various industries like healthcare, hospitality, education, and retail to incorporate unattended solutions into their operations. This event will involve experts sharing their expertise and strategies with attendees to enhance their businesses. It aims to explore the benefits of utilizing unattended solutions in different sectors.

data.title: Michael Moskal receives 2025 Five Star Wealth Manager Award for exceptional service at MGO & OneSeven.
data.description: Cleveland, July 31, 2025 - MGO and OneSeven have proudly revealed that Michael Moskal, a distinguished advisor at MGO, operating as a DBA of OneSeven, has received the prestigious 2025 Five Star Wealth Manager Award. This accolade underscores Michael's unwavering dedication to assisting clients in reaching their financial goals.

data.title: Inventor from N.C. creates Blue Pearl toilet splash guard to protect surfaces from urine splashes.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on July 31, 2025, an inventor from Yadkinville, N.C. announced the creation of a novel toilet seat attachment called the BLUE PEARL. This innovative product aims to prevent urine splashes from reaching the bathroom floor and other surfaces. The inventor shared that the inspiration behind the invention was the desire to create a protective barrier. The BLUE PEARL design is crafted to address this concern effectively.

data.title: Orthopedic spine surgeon Dr. Adam J. Schell opens Nebraska Spine + Pain Center satellite clinic on August 12, 2025 at CHI Health St. Mary's in Nebraska City.
data.description: In Omaha, Nebraska, the Nebraska Spine + Pain Center has shared news that Dr. Adam J. Schell, a board-certified orthopedic spine surgeon, will be inaugurating a satellite clinic on August 12, 2025. The new clinic will be situated within CHI Health St. Mary's facility.

data.title: KuCoin Ventures invests in Stable, first USDT-powered blockchain. Major investment in high-throughput Layer1 platform.
data.description: In Providenciales, Turks and Caicos Islands, on July 31, 2025, KuCoin Ventures, the investment division of well-known cryptocurrency exchange KuCoin, revealed a significant investment in Stable. Stable is recognized as the world's initial high-throughput Layer1 blockchain operated by USDT. This blockchain is committed to enhancing the efficiency and scalability of cryptocurrency transactions.

data.title: Seabourn announces 94-day "Pole to Pole: Grand Expedition" in 2027 spanning 82°N to 66°S, covering 20,500+ nautical miles.
data.description: A recently announced 94-day Expedition is set to journey from 82° North to 66° South, totaling over 20,500 nautical miles aboard Seabourn Venture. This unique expedition will showcase remote destinations, rare wildlife, and immersive experiences from the High Arctic down to Antarctica. The expedition is scheduled to depart from Seattle on July 31, 2025, offering a comprehensive exploration opportunity amidst stunning landscapes and diverse ecosystems.

data.title: France and UK strengthen nuclear defense coordination to counter Russian threats, marking a historic milestone. €800 billion to boost European military capabilities amid US concerns. France and UK create Nuclear Oversight Committee, share intelligence, and establish Nuclear Steering Group. Macron emphasizes alliance's importance in global security.
data.description: France and the UK have agreed to coordinate their nuclear defense efforts to enhance European deterrence against Russia. The Northwood Declaration, signed during French President Macron's visit to the UK, marks the first such coordination between the two nations. The European Union plans to deploy €800 billion for defense in response to Russia's aggression in Ukraine. The agreement includes sharing intelligence, developing contingency plans, and establishing a Nuclear Steering Group. The US views this as a significant step towards strengthening Europe's defense capabilities amid concerns about NATO burden-sharing. This collaboration signals increased European independence in defense and deterrence capabilities.

data.title: US dollar weakness driving profits surge for global companies like Magnificent Seven; EPS boost of 2-3% for S&P 500 with 10% DXY drop.
data.description: In 2025, Wall Street has hit record highs thanks to the Magnificent Seven stocks rebounding after a tariff-related selloff in April. However, a new earnings driver has emerged in the form of the weakening U.S. dollar. Goldman Sachs reports that the 10% year-to-date decline in the U.S. dollar index is set to significantly boost profits for companies with international exposure, especially the Magnificent Seven. These top companies earn 49% of their revenue overseas, compared to the S&P 500's 28% and small-cap Russell 2000's 20% average. Historically, a 10% drop in the dollar results in a 2-3% earnings-per-share boost for the S&P 500, potentially even higher for the Magnificent Seven including Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta Platforms, and Tesla. Full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: IU's Memorial Stadium upgraded with NFL-caliber turf and track by Hellas, with Matrix Helix® turf field and resurfaced track.
data.description: In Bloomington, Indiana, as of July 31, 2025, the Memorial Stadium at Indiana University has been upgraded with a new Matrix Helix® artificial turf field and track by Hellas, a TenCate Company. The newly renovated facility promises top-notch sports amenities with a modern playing field and improved track surface.

data.title: NAV Fund Services wins Best Administrator - Digital Assets at With Intelligence HFM Asia Services Awards in Hong Kong on July 3, 2025.
data.description: In Oakbrook Terrace, Illinois on July 31st, 2025, NAV Fund Services was honored with the title of Best Administrator for Digital Assets at the With Intelligence HFM Asia Services Awards 2025 in Hong Kong, held on July 3rd. This prestigious award ceremony aims to acknowledge and commend hedge fund service providers and NAV Fund Services stood out among competitors in the digital assets administration category.

data.title: AstraZeneca beats Q2 sales estimates, reports $14.46B revenue (+12% YoY), driven by oncology growth; affirms 2025 outlook.
data.description: AstraZeneca Plc, listed on NASDAQ under AZN, announced its second-quarter sales for 2025, totaling $14.46 billion, showing a 12% increase year-over-year (11% at constant currency). This figure slightly surpassed the consensus estimate of $14.07 billion. The growth was propelled by double-digit increases in oncology and biopharmaceuticals. The company's adjusted EPADS fell short of expectations at $1.08, lower than the anticipated $1.10. The adjusted EPS stood at $2.17 (1 ADR equals 2 Common Shares).Within AstraZeneca's portfolio, oncology (cancer) emerged as the primary revenue driver, contributing 44% to the total sales. Oncology sales surged by 18% (constant currency up 18%) to $6.31 billion. Tagrisso witnessed a 13% increase in sales to $1.81 billion (12% at CER), Imfinzi revenues rose by 27% (26% increase) reaching $1.46 billion, Calquence sales saw a 10% boost hitting $872 million, Lynparza sales grew by 13% (11% increase) to $838 million, and Enhertu revenue experienced a significant spike of 41% (42% at CER) amounting to $666 million.This period also saw AstraZeneca facing challenges as they fell short of the expected EPADS value. The full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: UnitedHealth Group reports Q2 earnings of $4.08 (down from $6.80), on revenues of $111.62 billion (up 13% YoY). CEO aims for growth in 2026.
data.description: UnitedHealth Group reported second-quarter 2025 earnings with adjusted earnings at $4.08, down from $6.80 last year. Revenues rose 13% year over year to $111.62 billion, missing the consensus of $111.69 billion. The Q2 medical care ratio increased 430 basis points to 89.4%, and operating cost ratio improved from 13.3% to 12.3%. Q2 revenue was $86.1 billion, up 17% year-over-year, with the segment serving 50 million people, an increase of 770,000 year-to-date. Earnings from operations were $2.1 billion, down from $4.0 billion in Q2 2024, with the margin dropping from 5.4% to 2.4%. UnitedHealth CEO Stephen Hemsley expressed optimism for future growth in 2026 and beyond.

data.title: Improving adds 2 new board members to advance 10-year vision; Vasu Sarangapani and Raghu Chandra join.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, in Dallas, Improving, a prominent provider of digital services and advocate of Conscious Capitalism, revealed the selection of Vasu Sarangapani and Raghu Chandra to its Board of Directors. This decision aligns with the company's dedication to achieving its 10-Year Vision.

data.title: AbbVie reports $15.42B sales in Q2 2025, misses consensus earnings at $2.97. Raises outlook for fiscal 2025.
data.description: AbbVie Inc. reported second-quarter 2025 sales of $15.42 billion, exceeding the consensus of $14.98 billion. Sales increased by 6.6% on a reported basis or 6.5% on an operational basis. The company's adjusted earnings were $2.97, up 12.1% year over year but lower than the consensus of $3.23. Global net revenues from the immunology portfolio were $7.63 billion, with Skyrizi sales up 62.2% to $4.42 billion, Rinvoq sales up 41.8% to $2.03 billion, and Humira sales down 58.1% to $1.18 billion. The oncology portfolio generated sales of $1.68 billion, with declining sales for Imbruvica and growing sales for Venclexta. The neuroscience portfolio had sales of $2.68 billion, with Vraylar net revenues up by 16.3% and Botox Therapeutic net revenues up by 14.1%. AbbVie also raised its fiscal 2025 adjusted earnings forecast to $11.88-$12.08 per share.

data.title: Cheez-It maker Kellanova reported Q2 profit down, EPS 94 cents (missed estimate of $1), with sales of $3.203 billion (0.3% decrease).
data.description: Kellanova (NYSE: K) posted second-quarter adjusted earnings per share at 94 cents, falling short of the expected $1 per share as estimated by analysts. The company, known for popular brands like Pringles, Cheez-It, and Pop-Tarts, along with Kellogg's Rice Krispies, generated quarterly sales of $3.203 billion. This figure, showing a decrease of 0.3% year over year, exceeded the Street's projection of $3.197 billion. In North America, both reported and organic net sales saw a 4% decline during Q2, attributed to reduced volume amidst persistent weakness in snacking and frozen product categories. Europe's reported net sales... Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.
